source: FDA 2024 ADC Guidance; loncastuximab tesirine BLA review
description: PBD dimers cause sequence-selective DNA minor groove alkylation producing interstrand crosslinks. Extremely potent (picomolar IC50) with very long DNA damage half-life. Very narrow therapeutic index. Used in: loncastuximab tesirine.

expected_findings.description: Bone marrow aplasia (severe — narrow therapeutic index)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any significant marrow aplasia = adverse. PBD ADCs have the narrowest TI of any payload class.
expected_findings.rationale: PBD interstrand crosslinks are exceptionally cytotoxic to dividing hematopoietic progenitors. Narrow TI means dose-proportional progression to aplasia.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any multilineage cytopenia = adverse for PBD class
expected_findings.rationale: DNA crosslinks affect all dividing progenitors. Pancytopenia expected at near-therapeutic doses.
Hepatocyte DNA damage from systemic PBD exposure.

expected_findings.description: GI mucosal necrosis (more severe than microtubule inhibitor ADCs)
expected_findings.rationale: Intestinal crypt cell DNA damage. More severe GI toxicity than microtubule inhibitor payloads.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any pulmonary histopathological finding = adverse for PBD class
expected_findings.rationale: Pulmonary endothelial and epithelial DNA damage from systemic PBD exposure.
